data.title: CoreWeave rises after results of largest-ever Nvidia Grace Blackwell GPU clusters
data.description:
Shares of CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) rose about 3% on Wednesday after the company, in collaboration with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and IBM (NYSE:IBM), delivered the largest-ever MLPerf Training v5.0 submission results for Nvidia's GB200 Grace Blackwell chips.

CoreWeave said 2,496 Blackwell graphics processing units, or GPUs, were running on CoreWeave's AI-optimized cloud platform.

The submission is the largest GB200 NVL72 cluster ever benchmarked under MLPerf, 34times larger than the only other submission from a cloud provider highlighting the large scale of CoreWeave's cloud platform for demanding AI workloads, according to CoreWeave.

CoreWeave noted that the submission achieved a breakthrough result on the largest and most complex foundational model in the benchmarking suite, Llama 3.1 405B, completing the run in 27.3 minutes.

CoreWeave's GB200 cluster achieved more than twice as faster training performance, compared to submissions from other participants across similar cluster sizes, according to the company.

"These MLPerf results reinforce our leadership in supporting today's most demanding AI workloads," said Peter Salanki, co-founder and chief technology officer at CoreWeave.

These results matter because they translate directly to faster model development cycles and an optimized total cost of ownership, according to CoreWeave.

For CoreWeave's customers, this could cut training time in half, scaling workloads efficiently, and training or deploying models more cost-effectively by using latest cloud technologies, months before their competitors, CoreWeave noted.

In April, CoreWeave — which went public in March — brought thousands of Grace Blackwell GPUs online, becoming the first cloud provider to make the new GPUs generally available at scale.

data.title: ECB June Meeting Preview: Rate cut expected as inflation falls below target
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) is expected to lower its deposit rate by 25 basis points to 2.00% at its June 6 meeting, as inflation in the eurozone slips below target and economic momentum remains weak. A sharp decline in both headline and core inflation in May has strengthened the case for monetary easing, with markets fully pricing in the rate cut.

Headline inflation for the region dropped to 1.9%, while core inflation fell to 2.3%, largely due to a notable slowdown in services inflation from 4.0% to 3.2%. This marks the first time inflation has dipped below the ECB’s 2% target since September 2023. With wage growth also easing and energy prices declining, the disinflationary trend appears to have solidified.

The ECB has already cut rates seven times since June 2023, and Thursday’s move is expected to be the first of potentially two further cuts this year. Economists expect the deposit rate to fall to 1.75% by year-end, as inflation remains subdued, and price pressures continue to fade.

However, uncertainty surrounding global trade tensions — particularly U.S. tariffs on European goods — complicates the outlook. While recent developments have temporarily eased concerns, the potential for retaliatory tariffs or further protectionist measures poses an upside risk to inflation. ECB officials are likely to tread carefully, avoiding firm commitments on the future path of rates.

ECB President Christine Lagarde is expected to adopt a cautious tone, emphasizing the need for a data-dependent approach amid ongoing external risks. Updated staff forecasts for inflation and growth will be closely watched, particularly in light of softening market-based inflation expectations.

While Thursday’s rate cut is highly anticipated, the ECB’s forward guidance and tone will be key in shaping market expectations for the rest of 2025.

data.title: Circle IPO set to price higher than marketed range after 25x oversubscription - report
data.description:
Stablecoin issuer Circle Internet Financial's (CRCL) upcoming initial public offering is likely to price above its marketed range of $27-$28 per share, after attracting demand over 25 times the shares on offer, according to a media report published Wednesday.

The company and selling shareholders would offer 32M shares at the top of the $27-$28 price range to raise $896M, according to its latest SEC filing. That's up from 24M shares at a $24-$26 range disclosed in an earlier filing.

The IPO is slated to price on Wednesday evening (New York time), Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

"Allocations of shares will be skewed toward investors with a strategy aimed at holding stock for the long term," the people added, noting that deliberations remain ongoing and Circle (CRCL) could still price the offering within the price range.

If priced at the top end of the range, Circle's (CRCL) market value would approach $6.2B, rising to roughly $7.2B on a fully diluted basis including stock-based comp and warrants, the article said. 

BlackRock (BLK), the world's largest asset manager, is reportedly planning to buy ~10% of the shares offered in Circle's (CRCL) IPO.

data.title: Hudbay halts Manitoba operations due to wildfires
data.description:
Hudbay Minerals (NYSE:HBM) said Wednesday it has temporarily suspended operations in Snow Lake, Manitoba, due to advancing wildfires and evacuation orders affecting Snow Lake, Flin Flon, and surrounding areas, maintaining only essential personnel authorized by emergency services.

Hudbay (NYSE:HBM) said it believes its infrastructure and facilities in both locations have a low risk of fire damage, and despite the temporary suspension, it still expects to achieve its FY 2025 guidance metrics for Manitoba operations due to strong YTD performance.

The company recently said the Toronto Stock Exchange approved its notice of intention to commence a normal course issuer bid for its common shares.

data.title: Microsoft shakes up leadership, brings LinkedIn leader to run Office: report
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) has shaken up some of its leadership, as the Windows maker told employees on Wednesday that LinkedIn CEO Ryan Roslansky will now run its Office group, Bloomberg reported.
  • In granting Roslansky more responsibility over business software such as Outlook and Word, it hopes it will see the deployment of more artificial intelligence tools, the news outlet added. Roslansky will report to Rajesh Jha, a Microsoft engineering executive in his capacity as Office leader and Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella in his role as LinkedIn CEO.
  • In addition to the leadership shuffle, Microsoft said that Charles Lamanna, who previously reported to Scott Guthrie, will now be a part of Jha's division. Lamanna oversees Dynamics 365 and some Copilot AI tools.
  • In its most recent quarter, Microsoft said revenue from its Productivity and Business Processes segment, which includes Office, was $29.9B, up 10% year-over-year. LinkedIn, which is also a part of the segment, saw its revenue rise 7%, while Dynamics products and cloud services revenue increased 11%, including a 16% growth in Dynamics 365 revenue growth.
  • Microsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Stocks slightly higher, Treasury yields slide as traders snap up bonds after weak data
data.description:
Wall Street on Wednesday saw muted gains while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2% in midday trade, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

U.S. private sector employment grew by 37K in May, missing the +110K consensus and cooling from the 62K increase in April, according to data released by ADP. 

Market participants reacted to the data by snapping up bonds on rising expectations that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates, sending U.S. Treasury yields lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 9 basis points to 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 6 basis points to 3.90%.

Donald Trump took to Truth Social to once again express frustration at Fed chair Jerome Powell. "'Too Late' Powell must now LOWER THE RATE. He is unbelievable!!! Europe has lowered NINE TIMES!" the President said. 

Trump also said Chinese President Xi Jinping has been “extremely hard” to strike a deal with, as the White House hinted at the possibility of the two leaders speaking this week amid escalating trade tensions.

"Many investors continue to look past the current trade confusion, convinced that it will all turn out fine in the end. And as things stand, nothing much should happen between now and July to upset markets, as it is next month when the high reciprocal tariffs would kick in, assuming the absence of any bilateral trade deals, while there also around six weeks until the start of the second quarter earnings season," David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation 

U.S. major averages ended Tuesday with solid gains as encouraging job openings data overshadowed a major intergovernmental organization downgrading its global and U.S. economic growth projections. 

One of the most important catalysts for the recovery was that JOLTS report, which showed the US labor market in a more robust position than previously thought, Reid added. 

The U.S. ISM Services PMI retreated to 49.9 in May from 51.6 in April.

Furthermore, the S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI advanced to 53.0 in May's final estimate, compared with the 52.1 flash reading and 50.6 in April.

data.title: Airbus rises amid reports of large-scale jet deal with China
data.description:
Airbus's (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) American depositary receipts rose more than 3% on Wednesday as speculation intensified around a potential blockbuster aircraft order from China, possibly involving hundreds of jets. The renewed momentum in negotiations comes ahead of a planned summit between European and Chinese leaders in Beijing next month.

Bloomberg News reported that discussions could result in an agreement for up to 300 Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) aircraft, including both narrowbody and widebody models, with the final number potentially reaching as many as 500.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) declined to comment to Bloomberg News on the report.

Industry insiders say talks have been ongoing for over a year, as Beijing weighs options to expand the fleets of domestic airlines amid heightened trade tensions with the U.S., home to Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) rival Boeing (BA). The discussions appear to build on conversations that began around Chinese President Xi Jinping’s state visit to France in May 2024, when a major order was expected but not finalized.

Sources told Reuters earlier this year that negotiations have fluctuated amid shifting political dynamics, particularly following changes in U.S. leadership and ongoing tariff disputes. The scale of the potential order has reportedly grown in recent months, especially as U.S.-China trade friction has made it difficult for Boeing to close deals in the Chinese market.

Historically, China has tied large jet orders to diplomatic events, using them as leverage in broader geopolitical engagements. While such negotiations are often timed to coincide with high-profile summits, sources caution that final decisions can be delayed and are never guaranteed. One person familiar with the talks noted that a deal could still be pushed to later in the year.

Chinese airlines are under mounting pressure to modernize their fleets after a lengthy freeze in major purchases by the country’s central procurement agency, which had paused new orders during the first wave of U.S.-China trade tensions under former President Trump.

Airbus, which expects Chinese air travel demand to grow at an annual rate of 5% over the next 20 years, stands to benefit significantly if the deal materializes. The potential Chinese order also follows a wave of activity in India, where Air India is exploring a purchase of around 200 aircraft and IndiGo recently expanded its existing Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) commitment.

As global travel demand continues to rebound, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) appears well positioned to gain ground in Asia, particularly as Boeing (BA) remains sidelined in one of the world's largest aviation markets.

data.title: Trump’s tax bill projected to leave 11M without insurance: CBO
data.description:
The nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office on Wednesday projected that President Donald Trump’s "One Big Beautiful Bill Act," which includes sweeping tax cuts and spending measures, will make nearly 11M Americans without health insurance.

The Republican budget bill narrowly cleared the House late last month by a single vote. The CBO released the report as the focus now turns to the Senate, where several Republican lawmakers have cited concerns about its potential to widen the U.S. budget deficit and introduce changes to Medicaid, a health insurance program jointly funded by the states and the U.S. government.

According to the report, nearly 7.8M Americans would lose their Medicaid coverage, while 1.4M people, including immigrants who can’t prove their legal status in the U.S., will also be deprived of health insurance due to the legislation. Much of the other coverage losses will come from the bill’s proposed changes to the Affordable Care Act’s exchanges.

Health insurers: UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Humana (HUM), CVS Health (CVS), Cigna (CI), Clover Health (CLOV), Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Centene (NYSE:CNC), Molina Healthcare (NYSE:MOH), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV),

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile continues to rally as investors eye the role Amazon and Jeff Bezos could play
data.description:
AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) extended its recent rally on Wednesday as investors continue to eye the company's relationship with Amazon (AMZN). The buzz went into overdrive after AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) board member Adriana Cisneros posted a picture on Instagram with Jeff Bezos titled "Amazing things are happening at AST & Science + @blueorigin". Notably, Cisneros was AST SpaceMobile's (ASTS) first-ever institutional investor and is currently a board member. 

Scotiabank analyst Andres Coello highlighted that there are major areas of opportunity where AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), Amazon (AMZN), and Blue Origin (BORGN) could collaborate, particularly regarding Project Kuiper, which intends to address the fixed broadband opportunity. 

"Could Jeff Bezos become an ASTS investor? This is a possibility widely discussed by investors and analysts, in no small measure because Bezos is one of the world's top space investors," wrote Coello.

It was noted that while Amazon (AMZN) intends to invest $13 billion to $16 billion in the Kuiper constellation of 3,236 satellites, the current market capitalization of ASTS stands at only $8.3 billion. Notably, the firm's revenue projection for ASTS is actually larger than for Kuiper at $30 billion by 2033 vs. the $25 billion estimated for Kuiper. Looking ahead, Scotiabank said it would not be surprised if both groups are exploring a larger agreement that could involve New Glenn's support for BB2 launches and possible commercial/equity agreements between ASTS and Amazon (AMZN).

Shares of AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) are up 5.3% in morning trading. Over the last week, the satellite manufacturer stock is up more than 15%.

data.title: Trump's 'big, beautiful bill' comes with a $2.42T price tag, CBO estimates
data.description:
The nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office estimated Wednesday that U.S. President Donald Trump's tax-and-spending package -- narrowly passed by House Republicans last month and now facing resistance in the Senate -- would add $2.42T to fiscal budget deficits over the next 10 years.

The bill would reduce the federal government's revenues by $3.67T and decrease outlays by $1.25T over the decade through 2034, according to the CBO's calculations.

The projections come as concerns about the sustainability of the U.S.'s fiscal path heat up.

On Tuesday, Just days after Elon Musk left Washington, DC, and his post heading the Department of Government Efficiency, the richest man in the world harshly criticized the “One Big Beautiful Bill" that Trump is urging Republican senators to back, calling it a "disgusting abomination."

Trump is set to gather with Senate Finance Republicans on Wednesday to talk through the bill.

Note that the $2.42T figure from the CBO excludes any dynamic scoring, meaning the deficit-increase projection does not factor in potential economic growth or other secondary effects from tax and spending changes.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has pushed back against growing worries about rising U.S. debt on the view that an acceleration in economic growth would help offset the expected revenue headwind from the bill.
